div.head: The Ambassador in Brazil (Morgan) to the Secretary of State

opener.dateline:
Rio de Janeiro, March 20, 1933—5 p.m.
[Received March 20—2:35 p.m.]

div.p: 34. Although in courtesy to the League of Nations the Brazilian Government has stated that it would be pleased to cooperate with the United States in carrying out the League’s last proposal regarding Leticia, the Foreign Office asks me to inform you that it doubts whether such an effort will lead to success. Brazil’s first effort having failed she is disinclined to associate herself with a second unless success is assured before the effort is made.
